mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating earnings upgrades and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices coinciding with higher policy rates compress real equity valuations and trigger defensive positioning, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns during active tightening phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of tightening episodes despite rising inflation and rates, or the joint signal shows no predictive power over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects strengthening aggregate demand, which improves near-term corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDP acceleration, or the signal fails to outperform a random walk over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in residential mortgage debt outstanding below 320 billion dollars signals a housing credit contraction that drags on economic activity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters following a debt decline below the threshold, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over baseline volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market health, supporting wage growth and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor indicators produce negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts during a late-cycle phase signal weakening economic momentum, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of late-cycle contraction episodes, or the joint supply-demand signal fails to predict downside moves better than a flat baseline.
